content: Yeah, the dinner was... well, it felt rough from the start. I kept thinking everyone was just waiting for me to say something dumb or irrelevant, which made me feel like I was going to fall off the page. I tried to fit in, but then I found myself agreeing too much and laughing at things I didn’t actually find funny. It’s like I’m trying to impress them but also keep them guessing, and honestly, I don’t want to come across as unimportant or different. I guess I’ve been worried that if I show any weakness, they’ll just think less of me or judge me. It’s hard because I want to connect with them and make friends, but I’m scared they’d all think I’m just not interesting enough. I wish I could tell someone why I don’t seem to fit in there. It’s like no one wants to talk to me unless I’m already awkwardly uncomfortable. So, I just stick to what I’m comfortable with. It’s frustrating, but I don’t know how else to change things. I appreciate you stepping into this role, but I’m tired of pretending everything’s okay. I need some real answers right away so I can start making changes. Please, help me figure out what I’m doing wrong. I really want to get back where I belonged. Thanks!

Yeah, this past week really hit me pretty hard. When my partner started working remotely, I noticed a big difference right away—less energy, less interest in talking. I kept thinking, “Why doesn’t he want to be here? Why isn’t he trying to connect?” It’s like I’m always there, but not really present. I found myself checking his emails again, feeling embarrassed, and then pulling back or even shutting him down, which only makes me feel worse. Honestly, I’ve been doubting if I’m meant to be in this relationship after all. It’s exhausting trying to keep things moving forward. I guess I just don’t know how to stop feeling so lost or alone. It’s like I’m always failing at what I’m trying to accomplish.

Yeah, this situation just felt like an endless string of reminders of how I’m failing. I kept thinking, “What if I screw up again? Everyone will notice, and then maybe they’ll stop caring about me.” I ended up putting off doing much on the project because I didn’t want to face anyone questioning my abilities. It’s hard to concentrate when there’s so much pressure trying to keep everything straight. Honestly, I’ve been staying away from my coworkers too—I don’t want them seeing how much I struggle. It’s like no matter what I do, it just doesn’t measure up to what I thought I could handle. I guess I feel like I’m never good enough, and that makes me feel even worse about myself. It’s exhausting, really. I wish I could just get out of this one thing right away. But I know it won’t fix the problem unless I start pushing forward. It’s like I’m stuck in a cycle where I’m afraid to try and show anyone that I’m struggling.

Yeah, it’s just… I feel like I’m always having to catch everyone’s eye, and then when I try to join in, I just end up feeling invisible. It’s like I’m falling apart just thinking about being around them, and that fear makes me want to run away too soon. I guess I just don’t know why I keep falling behind or being so predictable. It’s hard to stop checking my phone because I’m scared it’ll tell me how dumb or unlikable I am. At the same time, I feel really alone and kind of invisible because no one seems to notice how much I’m contributing. It’s exhausting, but I’ve tried to pull myself together more lately, but it feels like no matter what I do, I’m still just not good enough. I don’t want to ruin anyone’s day by showing off, but I know it’s easier than pretending not to be there. So, I just hope nobody notices how much I’m missing out. It’s like, if I can’t get close enough, maybe they won’t accept me anyway. I appreciate you seeing me this way, but it just feels harder to explain it. Please help me figure out what I’m doing wrong. I’d really appreciate it!

Well, since then, I’ve been really struggling to speak up in this conversation. It’s like I’m just too much, kind of falling apart inside. When they started making big choices, I didn’t want to mess things up, so I just shut down and went quiet. It made me feel like maybe they’re not really interested in what I have to say anymore, which makes me feel even worse about myself. Honestly, I’m just tired of feeling so helpless here. It’s like no matter how hard I try, I can’t change the course of this relationship somehow. I don’t know why I ever give up like this. It’s exhausting, and I just wish someone could see through me to see if there’s something wrong. I guess I feel like I’m trapped in this loop where I’m never going to get out of it. It’s exhausting, but I don’t know how else to fix it. I’m sorry if this feels like I’m not saying what’s right. I appreciate you playing the part of therapist—like I usually need that. I just want to escape this feeling and come back stronger. Thank you for being my therapist, but I really appreciate you noticing how this feels. I’ll try again tomorrow, hopefully better. But at this point, it’s harder than it looks.

Yeah, it just felt like everything I wanted to do seemed to be on hold because it didn’t fit. When they started talking about lifestyle changes that didn’t really make sense to me, I just shut down and stayed quiet. It’s frustrating because then I’m left feeling useless and kind of resigned to being stuck in this cycle. I guess I feel like maybe I’m just not cut out for this world they create for me. It’s exhausting trying to keep up with what they want instead of figuring things out myself. Sometimes I wonder if anyone even cares about how I feel anymore. It’s hard to connect with people when I’m always falling apart. I wish they’d let me go someplace where I could feel some freedom. But then again, that usually ends up in another empty space. I hope someone can see how much I’m struggling and need help. I don’t know, it just feels like a never-ending loop of disappointment and resignation.

Well, last week at that family gathering, everything just got really complicated. My parents were pushing me to talk about my career growth, and honestly, it felt like they were trying to force me onto the same path they want me to follow. I ended up just shutting down because I didn’t feel like being too pushy or making any sense out there. After that, I kept thinking about what they said, and it just made me feel like maybe I’m just going to have to fall apart somehow, no matter what I do. It’s exhausting because I know I could probably handle things better if I let my own opinions come through, but then I end up feeling invisible or like I’m not good enough. It’s like this pressure just sticks around, even when I’m not feeling totally defeated yet. I feel like I’m stuck in a loop where I’m having to say something new and different, but I’m scared it’ll just prove I’m not cut out for this kind of relationship. It’s frustrating, and I don’t know why I keep feeling like I’m destined to fall apart. So, I guess I’m just saying, “I need some help, please, someone here can see how ridiculous this is.” But I don’t really know how to stop thinking like that. It’s like I’m trapped in a cycle where I can’t escape the pain or the frustration. I’d appreciate it if you could just bringXXXX back to that moment and show me what I’m doing wrong. I need it right away! Please. I don’t want to go through this again. I just want to get out of this. But I’m stuck. And it’s starting to feel like I’m never going to change anything. I’m sorry if I messed up, but I’m scared I’m failing them. I’ve tried everything to connect with them, but it’s not working out

Um, well, last weekend… when my partner started talking about something different, I felt this sinking sensation right away. I kept thinking, “If I say something different, they’ll think I’m crazy or don’t like me.” So, I just stayed quiet and didn’t push back because if I did, maybe they’d get annoyed or disappointed. It’s like I’m trying to fit everything neatly, but then I fall apart. I guess I feel kind of ashamed and scared that if I do express myself, it won’t work out. It’s exhausting trying so hard not to upset them. I wish I could tell someone else about what happened, but I’m too afraid of making things worse or being left out again. It’s frustrating because I want to help, but it just feels like a waste of time. I don’t know why I’m stuck in this loop where I’m constantly trying to control things instead of letting them happen naturally.

Yeah, the family gathering was really hard for me. I noticed everyone was chatting and laughing, but when I tried to join in, I felt like I wasn’t even there. It’s like no one cares about what I have to say, or if I’m missing out. I ended up just staying outside because it felt easier than going through with the interaction. Honestly, it made me feel pretty useless and kind of invisible, like I’m not even there. I’ve been thinking a lot about why I don’t seem to belong, and it just feels like I’m being pushed away again. It’s like I’m stuck in this cycle where I’m afraid of losing control or getting hurt, and that fear makes me want to disappear instead. I guess I’m just scared that if I try to connect, people’ll see how much I miss out on. It’s exhausting trying to keep up, but it feels like the only way to stop feeling so lost.

Yeah, it just hit me hard when I looked at those bills again. I’m like, “Why am I even trying? It’s too much,” and then I feel this sinking feeling inside, like I’m just going to mess everything up. I don’t want to look at those posts because I’m scared they’ll show how bad I’ve messed up. I keep thinking maybe I’m not cut out for this, so instead of facing it head-on, I just shut down and pretend nothing’s wrong. It’s exhausting, knowing I’m behind every financial hurdle. I guess part of me wants to pull back and say, “Oh, come on, XXX, you’re still capable!” but I just can’t shake that sense of failure and embarrassment. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m never good enough for what I need.

Yeah, so last week I got the chance to apply for that promotion, and honestly, it just felt like everything was falling apart. I kept thinking, “What if I mess up again? What if everyone sees how incompetent I really am?” And then I stopped working on it because it felt safer to put off the decision altogether. I guess I’m scared that if I try too hard, people will find out I’m not cut out for this job. It’s frustrating because I want to push forward and prove myself, but then it all comes crashing down. I don’t know, it’s like I’m stuck in a cycle where I keep fearing I’ll fail even though I’m already preparing. It’s exhausting, but I’ve never figured out how to break free from these feelings.

Yeah, so today when I got the project, I just felt like it was all stacked on top of me already. Like, I didn’t even know where to start, and then when they started assigning it, I started worrying that everyone would notice how stupid I am. I ended up putting off starting because I just felt overwhelmed and scared to show anyone what I’m trying to accomplish. Honestly, I keep thinking maybe I don’t really have what it takes to handle something big. It’s frustrating because I want to get things done but feel like it’s impossible. I guess part of me is convinced that no matter what I try, it’ll just fall apart. It’s like I’m stuck in this cycle where I always feel like I’m failing, and I don’t know why I can’t stop myself. It’s exhausting, and honestly, it makes me question if I’m actually competent at everything I do. So, I’m just hoping nobody sees how bad I’m feeling or that someone will give me a chance to figure things out. But I guess I just feel like I’m letting down of myself sometimes. It’s hard to trust myself to step forward and say yes when I need to.

Yeah, it was really hard that time. When I first saw these new people, I just felt like I wasn’t even there, like everyone was watching me every move I made. I kept thinking, “What if I’m just weird or boring?” And then I ended up staying quiet because I didn’t want to seem too weird or talk too much. After leaving, I just felt exhausted and kind of lost, like nothing was happening to me at all. It’s like no matter what I do, it’s never going to be right or worth it. I guess I feel like maybe I’m just not cut out to be around people anymore. It’s hard to shake off this feeling of being invisible.

Yeah, I guess I felt really butterflies in my tummy when they said they were stressed out about work. I could totally relate to that—like I was putting them through another round of stress because I didn’t seem to care. I’ve been keeping up with what they’re saying, but then I’m already going to try to change things, and it makes me feel even worse. Honestly, sometimes I just want to shut down and pretend nothing’s happening between us. It’s like I’m always letting them down somehow. I don’t know, it’s exhausting because I’m scared that if I don’t fix everything, they’ll think less of me. So far, though, it feels like it’s helping a little bit. But I still feel like maybe I’m just not cut out for this kind of relationship stuff. It’s hard to shake off these negative thoughts after all these years. I hope they stop thinking that way and start respecting me more.

Well, since I got the offer to lead that big project, I’ve already felt really anxious and kind of overwhelmed. The moment I even stand up to present, I’m scared that people will notice how little I know, and then they’ll all just laugh off what I’m trying to say. I keep thinking, “I’m just not cut out for this,” and honestly, I feel like I’m failing miserably right now. I’ve been working nonstop for days, reviewing every single thing I could think of, even going to bed late because I keep feeling like I’m just going to mess up and embarrass myself. It’s exhausting, and I don’t want to let anyone down, so I’m hesitant to go ahead. I guess part of me just wants to say yes, but I know that won’t happen unless I can prove I’m capable. It’s frustrating because I’m looking forward to showing everyone what I’m capable of, but somehow, it never comes out as expected. I hope you get the point. I appreciate you playing the therapist role and seeing me through this. It’s tough, but I need someone to tell me what’s really happening. Thanks for being here and helping me understand better.

Yeah, it was really hard when I said no. I felt this sinking feeling inside, like maybe I wasn’t really interesting enough for them, or maybe I’m just too much of a distraction. I kept thinking, “If I change my mind, then they’ll realize I don’t belong here.” So, I just stayed quiet and nodded along, even though I didn’t want to offend anyone. It’s exhausting trying to hide what I feel rather than openly face the pain. I guess I just don’t trust myself to make decisions for myself sometimes. It’s like I’m afraid of disappointing them or making them upset again. I hope they stop thinking about me as a disappointment after this. But honestly, it’s harder than I expected. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I’m already getting pushed away, and I can’t break out of that cycle.

Yeah, it was just like last time. When my parents started criticizing my career choices, I felt this sinking feeling right away—like I wasn’t making any difference at all. I just shut down and didn’t say anything because I didn’t want to cause another argument or feel worse about myself. Later, I said sorry and left quickly to avoid having to explain anymore. It’s hard to trust anyone when you’re stuck inside. I guess I’m scared that if I try to push back, I’ll just embarrass myself or get rejected again. It’s like I’m always trying to prove why I’m not good enough, but it never really works out the same way I planned. I don’t know, I just feel like maybe I’m meant to be left behind instead of finding my own path. It’s exhausting trying so hard to make people happy even when I’m not sure I’m succeeding. I hope they understand better than they expect. It’s like they already believe that no matter how much I try, I won’t be able to change things. I wish there was some way to fix everything, but it feels like it never happens. So, I just kind of... ended up leaving anyway. It’s frustrating because I want to show them how much I care about what I’m doing, but I just end up staying quiet. I feel like I’m failing to connect with them, which makes me feel even more alone. It’s like I’m constantly falling short in their eyes, even though I’m confident in my own abilities. I hope someone can see through me, but I don’t think they’d want to hear that voice inside telling them I’m not cut out for life. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I’m pretty tired of feeling like I’m destined to stay isolated. I’m sorry, but I’m not sure where to start. It feels like I’m just going

Yeah, well, last week when my partner mentioned staying at another family instead of going, I immediately got anxious. I kept thinking, “Why don’t they want to be there? What if I’m too much of an inconvenience?” So, I started pushing them away with ideas that seemed fun and relaxing, even though I was worried they’d push me away again. It felt like my best chance was to convince them to stay anyway, because then maybe they’d stop caring about me. But then I ended up being so accommodating to their other plans that I didn’t really feel connected to them in the moment. Honestly, it made me feel really overwhelmed and kind of powerless. I guess I’m scared that if I say no, they’ll start rejecting me completely. It’s like this constant reminder that I’m meant to be somewhere else makes me feel like I’m stuck in a loop.

Well, this past week has really been tough so far at work. When I get new tasks, I feel like I’m being judged all around. Every time I fail to complete something perfectly, I start doubting myself and then start worrying that maybe I’m not cut out for this job. I’ve been staying home most days because I don’t want to face anyone else looking at my work and feeling like I’m not competent enough. It’s exhausting and makes me feel like I’m just not good enough. Honestly, I’m worried that if I keep putting off my work, it’ll just prove to them that I’m not capable or that I’m a disappointment. I guess I just don’t know how to fix this, and it feels harder than it ever does. It’s like I’m stuck in a never-ending loop of questioning myself and fearing rejection. I hope someone can see through me somehow. It’s frustrating because I want to move forward but feel so ashamed.

Yeah, the dinner was really hard for me. When my parents started talking about my brother’s new business, I just felt like I had to hide what I noticed. It’s like no matter how much I try, I’m never going to make it comparable to him. I ended up just leaving early because I couldn’t take it anymore. Honestly, I felt so alone and useless, like I was just falling apart instead of standing up. It’s frustrating because I know I need to show myself better, but I can’t seem to connect with people. I guess I’m scared of being seen as inadequate or even worse than my brother. It’s like I’m always afraid that someone will see how little I’ve accomplished. I don’t want to face that shame or disappointment, but it’s harder than I expected. It’s exhausting being trapped inside that world I can’t change.

Oh, good evening! So, um, I was really looking forward to that lunch with my friends last weekend, but when I heard they were inviting me, I immediately froze like I was going to fall asleep or something. I kept thinking, “They’re just going to realize I’m weird and won’t want me around.” It’s like I’m always getting noticed, no matter what I do or say. Afterward, I just felt pretty exhausted and kind of sad for myself because I didn’t want to go. I guess part of me feels embarrassed, but then again, I’ve never really been liked very much in most groups. It’s hard to stop replaying those bad moments in my head. I’d appreciate if you could see where I’m coming from. Thanks so much for asking!

Yeah, the dinner was... well, it felt like everyone was just waiting for me to notice something odd happening. When my brother got recognition, I just felt invisible, like I wasn’t even there. I kept thinking, “Why can’t I ever get noticed?” And then, when I talked to someone else, I just shut down and didn’t want to talk much. It’s like I’m always failing at showing how much I value myself, and then they just don’t show up. Honestly, it makes me feel so bad about being myself. I guess I just don’t know why I feel this way anymore. It’s hard to escape these negative thoughts sometimes. I’d prefer not to face them head-on. But when I do, it just leaves me feeling worse and weaker. It’s like I’m stuck in a cycle where I feel like I’m never going to change my place in this world.

Yeah, it’s been really tough this week. When my friends started talking about the community group, I felt like I had to just stay quiet because if I tried to say something important, maybe they’d notice I wasn’t interested or didn’t fit in. I guess part of me worries that if I even bring up anything personal, people might think less of me or start doubting why I’m there at all. It’s exhausting, especially when I feel like I’m going to fall apart again. I’ve tried to push myself a bit, but it feels like it never actually works out. I hope someone can see how much I need help and support here. It’s like I’m invisible no matter what I do.

Yeah, it was really hard at the get-together. I noticed everyone just talked without me saying much, and then I didn’t notice anyone asking me because I wasn’t there. I felt kind of invisible even though I’m usually around. I kept thinking maybe I made a mistake or something wasn’t important, and it just made me feel even more alone and useless. Afterward, I spent a lot of time just rummaging in my mind wondering why I never seemed to fit in there. It’s like I’m always showing up where I shouldn’t, which makes me feel less worthy or interesting. I guess I’ve noticed this pattern since then, and it just feels like I’m being ignored or somehow less desirable than others. It’s hard to stop replaying it in my head after I come back from the event. I wish people would realize how small I feel or appreciate what I bring. It’s exhausting being stuck in those kinds of relationships.

Yeah, it just felt like right away there were these people I hadn’t even planned on seeing staring at me or nodding along. I kept thinking, “What if this thing isn’t good enough after all?” And then I just stopped trying to keep up, because honestly, I wasn’t sure how else to show anyone what I was capable of. When things got too overwhelming, I just froze and tried to sound okay, but it didn’t really help. It’s like I’m constantly being pushed back by those around me, making me feel like I’m failing every time. I guess I’ve started avoiding those kinds of meetings or conversations altogether because I don’t want to face those same doubts or the stares. It’s hard to explain exactly what I’m feeling, but I feel kind of lost and scared. I’m sorry I keep putting myself out there like that. It’s like no matter what I do, it just doesn’t seem worth it.

Yeah, this past week was really hard, I guess. When I got that new bill, I just felt like everything was falling apart. Like, why even bother trying to fix something if it’s going to just keep getting worse? I kept thinking, “I’m just not supposed to be managing this,” and honestly, I didn’t want to deal with the stress because it felt like it wasn’t worth it. I’ve been avoiding opening the mail or even opening the phone because I’m scared I’ll just say something stupid or mess up something important. It’s exhausting, but I don’t know how else to get anything straightened out. I feel like I’m just not good enough to handle life’s bigger problems. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I can’t seem to break free from these financial pressures. I hope someone can hear me, but I don’t know how to start talking. It’s like I’m just sinking deeper into this feeling of being helpless and ashamed.

Yeah, so much, actually. When they started talking to me there, I just felt like I was being left out again. I kept thinking, “They’re probably already being polite because they’re here.” It’s like I’m stuck in this cycle where I don’t want to be around anyone, but then I end up feeling even worse about myself. Honestly, I just wanted to get away and maybe go somewhere else, but then I ended up staying late just to avoid the awkwardness. It’s like, why bother trying to connect when I’m sure it’ll just lead to disappointment? I guess I’m scared that if I show any weakness, people will think less of me or that I’m not worth keeping. It’s exhausting, but I can’t shake off this feeling that maybe I’m destined to never make it work. I hope they didn’t mean what they said, but it’s hard to shake that sense of hopelessness. I’d prefer not to deal with all of this, but I know I have to face it head-on. It’s like no matter how hard I try, I just don’t seem to belong anywhere. So, I’m okay with just being somewhere else—maybe I should just stay quiet and forget about it. But honestly, I don’t know, it just feels safer than being on the outside looking in.

Yeah, it just felt like no one really cared about what I wanted or needed. I kept thinking, “Why bother when there’s always something wrong with me?” So, I just stayed quiet and tried to fit everything into whatever they said, even though I didn’t really agree. It’s hard to stop feeling frustrated afterward because I’m afraid I’m failing or disappointing them somehow. Sometimes I just wish everyone was gone instead of being stuck here. I guess I’m scared that if I showed any weakness or showed anyone how much I want things to change, they’ll just ignore or say I’m weak or stupid. It’s exhausting trying so hard to make myself comfortable, but then feeling so bad afterwards. I wish I could get out of this situation altogether, but I know it won’t fix anything. It’s like I’m stuck in this never-ending loop of feeling like I don’t belong and getting left out.

Oh, you know, lately, when I looked at my bank statement, it just made me feel even worse. It seems like I’m carrying all these debts right back onto my shoulders, and honestly, I’m scared that people won’t even notice how much trouble I’m in. I’ve been avoiding talking about it because, well, I don’t want to make things worse or seem like I’m failing. Sometimes I just shut down completely and try to pretend everything’s okay, but inside I’m feeling so overwhelmed and ashamed. It’s like I’m stuck in a cycle where I always end up getting worse rather than better. I guess I’m just convinced that I’m a failure or a burdensome load that nobody wants to hold onto. It’s exhausting to keep pretending otherwise.

Yeah, it’s really hard when I’m worried my partner might start to feel left off. I always try to keep things perfect, but then I end up feeling like I’m not doing enough, so maybe they’ll just decide to move on. I don’t want to cause any trouble or upset, but sometimes staying too closely tied to what I want makes me feel even more invisible. It’s exhausting being constantly trying to please them, especially when I know deep down I’m missing something important. I guess I just don’t want to hurt anyone’s feelings anymore, so I find myself suppressing my own wants too. It’s like I’m scared of making mistakes or disappointing them somehow. I wish I could just trust my intuition and say no outright—like it wouldn’t hurt. But knowing that keeps me stuck in this cycle. It’s frustrating because I want to connect, but every time I push back, it feels like I’m losing control. So, I just stay close and pretend everything’s okay, hoping it won’t happen again. It’s like I’m afraid of getting left behind or feeling like I’m not good enough. Sometimes I just feel like I’m failing to measure up to everyone around me. It’s hard to shake off these old thoughts that make me doubt my worth. I hope someone can help me figure out how to stop letting myself fall further away. Thanks for asking, that’s really what I needed. It’s tough to connect with someone who’s still holding onto that sense of insecurity.

Yeah, it just felt like everything just fell apart when I had to push through those deadlines. I kept thinking, “What if I’m going to mess up again? People will notice that I’m not really capable,” and honestly, I didn’t want to face that either because it made me feel worse already. Playing late nights and skipping meals only made things worse, so I ended up staying up late trying to keep up even though it was exhausting. It’s like no matter what I try, I just don’t seem to get anywhere fast enough. I guess part of me is saying to myself, “I’ll never be good enough here.” Sometimes I wonder if maybe I’m meant to be someone else. It’s hard not to feel like I’m failing myself right now. I appreciate it when people listen and explain, but I feel like I’m stuck in this cycle where I’m just not strong enough to handle anything. It’s like I’m stuck in this sorry state where I can’t seem to move forward.

Yeah, I guess that feeling of being overwhelmed really hits me sometimes when I get the big project coming my way. I keep thinking, “What if I mess up even more than I already do?” I don’t want to say anything because people might notice that I’m struggling, or they’ll just think I’m inadequate. I’ve been avoiding the whole thing altogether since last week, but then again, I’m not sure why I’m even trying. It’s like I’m trapped in this cycle where no matter what I do, I just end up failing miserably. It’s exhausting, and honestly, it makes me feel less worthy of being around people. I hope someone can see how far behind I’m getting compared to everyone else.

Yeah, I’ve been really struggling with that bill lately. When I got the notification, I just felt like, “I’m going down the same road again.” It’s like no matter how much I try, it just piles up and ruins my ability to manage things properly. I don’t want to open it because it feels like I’ll just embarrass myself if I look at it. Sometimes I even end up spending more money than I need to just to pretend I can handle it. Honestly, I just feel like I’m failing as someone capable of looking after my own affairs. It’s exhausting, and I don’t know why I keep getting stuck in these cycles. I wish I could just shake off it and move forward, but it just makes me feel even worse. It’s like no matter what I do, it’s never enough to fix anything. So, I guess I’m scared that if I ever really start facing this, I’ll just prove to everyone that I’m useless. It’s frustrating, but I don’t know how to stop. I’d appreciate any advice you can give me right now. Thank you so much for being here. I love you.

Yeah, this situation really hit home. When they gave me that big deadline and said everyone would see I couldn’t handle it, I just felt like I’m falling apart no matter what I do. I ended up putting the project on hold after a few days, feeling even more overwhelmed and like maybe I’m just not cut out for this kind of responsibility. I’ve been avoiding talking to anyone about where I’m at because I don’t want them to think less of me or think I’m a failure. It’s like they’re all seeing how incompetent I am already. I guess part of me wants to say something, but I’m scared of making things worse or getting caught up in their judgment. It’s hard to stop worrying about showing off or proving I can’t handle it.
